name: William Grant Still
abstract: William Grant Still Jr. (May 11, 1895 – December 3, 1978) was an American composer of nearly two hundred works, including five symphonies, four ballets, nine operas, and more than thirty choral works, art songs, chamber music, and solo works.

works_created.t: Suite for Violin and Piano: 2. Mother and Child. Slowly and expressively
5 Miniatures: 1. I Ride an Old Paint
5 Miniatures: 3. Jesus is a Rock in the Weary Land
Songs of Separation: If you should go
Danzas de Panama: 5. Cumbia y Congo

about_quotes.speaker: Jang
about_quotes.evidence: I was introduced to the Afro-American music symphony by William Grant Still

about_quotes.speaker: Gerald Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: So in the meantime they introduced me to William Grant Still. ... Now I met him and he knew who I was, like I said. ... But he had met me in person.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gerald Wilson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Artie Shaw
about_quotes.evidence: I sat down with Bill Still, and I wrote part of the arrangement—William Grant Still—and he wrote the other part.
about_quotes.doc_title: Artie Shaw — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Thomas
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER: We did a ... a fellow you might remember by the name of William Grant Still (Steele). ... INTERVIEWER: Him I didn't know. I met him in later years but Joe Jordan conducted the band out there and added to my respect of Joe Jordan.

about_quotes.speaker: Tom McIntosh
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER: Did you ever listen to folks like William Grant Still... [INTERVIEWEE] No, the only thing I…Duke Ellington was allowed to...
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom McIntosh — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: William Grant Still, whom I had interviewed and talked at length to out in Los Angeles.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
